options.responseData: ={{ JSON.stringify({ ok: $('Build Response').first().json.ok, diagnosis: $('Build Response').first().json.diagnosis, error: $('Build Response').first().json.error || null }) }}

nodes.name: Copilot Troubleshoot Webhook (DEACTIVATED)
nodes.notes: DEACTIVATED. Body: { token, error_id (optional), description (optional) }. Exactly one of error_id or description required.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6', max_tokens: 1500, system: 'You are Crystallux Troubleshoot Copilot. Diagnose the error(s) the operator is investigating and propose a safe, specific fix. Rules: (a) only propose SQL UPDATEs that touch scan_errors (resolved flag via mark_error_resolved RPC) or non-destructive noop actions; (b) never propose INSERT/DELETE/DROP/ALTER; (c) if the root cause is outside the database (credential issue, workflow bug, third-party outage), say so explicitly and suggest the operator route. Return ONLY a JSON object: {"root_cause": string, "affected_records": number, "severity": "low" | "medium" | "high" | "critical", "suggested_fix": string (human-readable), "sql_to_execute_if_any": string | null, "dashboard_link": string | null}. No prose outside JSON.', messages: [{ role: 'user', content: $json.userContent }] }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ admin_user: 'mary', action_type: 'troubleshoot_initiated', input_text: ($('Validate + Route').first().json.description || 'error_id:' + ($('Validate + Route').first().json.error_id || 'unknown')), panel_context: 'alerts', generated_content: $json.diagnosis || null, result_summary: $json.ok ? 'diagnosed' : 'failed', success: $json.ok === true, error_message: $json.error || null, created_at: new Date().toISOString() }) }}
